clean_tree_evidence_gate.description: _run_fixture always runs (even dirty, for local debugging) and honestly reports evidence_gate.code_dirty/comparable/claim_grade. main()'s --mode fixture path refuses to write docs/design/ from a dirty/unknowable tree unless --allow-dirty is passed explicitly; even then the artifact stays marked comparable=false/claim_grade=false.

deferred: From-scratch clean git-clone regeneration (this sandbox regenerates from the committed HEAD of the working checkout instead, verified via code_dirty=false/code_commit rather than a separate clone).
A broader sweep of additional call-order permutations beyond the two exercised (reordered probes, inserted extra probe).
training_batch_order namespace exercised end-to-end (no shuffling fixture exists yet to exercise it).

determinism_report.note: Two isolated in-process fixture executions (run A/B), a call-order permutation (recursive-first shape probes), and an inserted-extra-harmless-probe permutation are compared field by field after excluding version_stamp.stamped_at. A distinct training-corruption-seed run is also compared and is *expected* to differ only in losses/post_update_verification/deep_supervision_metrics/rng_contract -- any other field changing under a different corruption seed would be a real namespace-isolation defect, listed in different_training_corruption_seed_unexpected_changes.

linear_context: SLM-138 adversarial audit ledger (follow-on to RSC-A01/RSC-A02)
note: Fixture RNG determinism/reproducibility fix only -- no model-quality interpretation is attached. SLM-237/238's training_loss objective semantics (validate_recursive_depth_supervision, recursive_depth_aux_mode, RecursiveObjectiveContractV2) are unchanged; this is RNG plumbing around the SLM-138 fixture.

namespace_notes.control_only: Any other incidental draw; used by the fixture's optional extra-harmless-probe test hook.
namespace_notes.shape_probe_context: Isolated via torch.random.fork_rng (isolated_draw) -- provably leaves the outer stream untouched.
namespace_notes.shape_probe_inputs: Isolated via torch.random.fork_rng (isolated_draw) -- provably leaves the outer stream untouched.
namespace_notes.training_batch_order: Reserved for future batch shuffling; declared but not exercised (fixed single batch, no shuffling).
namespace_notes.training_corruption: Explicitly (re)seeds both the global torch RNG and model._rng immediately before each training_loss call.

root_cause: The fixture drew synthetic forward-shape-probe tensors (torch.randint/torch.randn) from the global torch RNG between model construction and training_loss, whose internal corruption sampling (_mask_targets) also reads the global RNG unconditionally with no reseed of its own. Inserting/reordering a probe shifted the corruption draws training_loss saw, changing per-depth values in the low decimal places -- exactly Kimi's reproduction report. The checked historical artifact also recorded code_dirty=true.

second_rng_source_found_during_this_work.description: TwoTowerModel keeps a persistent per-instance random.Random(config.seed) (self._rng) that _mask_targets also reads (predictable-token fallback, mixed-pattern span selection). Restoring only the global torch RNG state between two training_loss calls on the same model reproduces a *different* loss the second time.
second_rng_source_found_during_this_work.fix: RngCheckpoint/seed_training_corruption capture and restore both the global torch RNG state and (when a model is passed) the model's self._rng state together.
